title: A Coyote Sank Into 4-Foot Mud on Long Island. A Rescuer Waded In.
script: Animal rescuers in St. James, New York, got an urgent call Thursday — a coyote had wandered into a Long Island marsh and sunk into 4-foot-deep mud, barely keeping its nose above the surface. Rescuer Frankie Floridia waded into the mud, caught the coyote with a catch pole, and a neighbor used a rope to pull them both to shore. The coyote had a broken leg. It is now in recovery.
